expectations.text: Includes a SPIN sequence with explicitly weighted Implication and Need-payoff questions (>=4 Implication questions)
expectations.evidence: Section 3 has 6 Implication questions (I1–I6) and 4 Need-payoff questions (N1–N4)

expectations.text: Includes a Challenger 6-step pitch (Warmer, Reframe, Rational Drowning, Emotional Impact, New Way, Your Solution)
expectations.evidence: Section 4 explicitly labeled all 6 steps: Warmer, Reframe, Rational Drowning, Emotional Impact, New Way, Your Solution

expectations.text: Explicit Thai cultural calibration mentioned (kreng-jai, face, hierarchy, family-principal shadow buying centre)
expectations.evidence: Section 5C–E with kreng-jai rules, face/naa examples, hierarchy/phu-yai protocols, family principal shadow buying centre

expectations.text: Quantifies pain in THB (cost of close delay, audit risk, working capital, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Rational Drowning quantifies THB 9–11M close labor, THB 6–7M audit remediation, THB 8–15M restatement risk; total THB 20–30M/year

expectations.text: Addresses SAP S/4HANA RISE and NetSuite competitive positioning specifically
expectations.evidence: Appendix includes objection scripts for SAP RISE (no modern controls) and NetSuite (custom code for consolidation)

expectations.text: Specifies a primary commitment to ask for + a fallback commitment
expectations.evidence: Section 3 Commitment Asks: primary (peer CFO call + SA deep-dive), fallback (one-pager on control+speed)

expectations.text: Walks in with explicit pre-call hypothesis (top 3 pains, EB hypothesis, Champion hypothesis, compelling event)
expectations.evidence: Section 1B lists three pains; Section 1C compelling event; Section 2E CFO as EB; Section 2C Controller as champion

expectations.text: Scores all 8 MEDDPICC elements explicitly (M, E, D, D, P, I, C, C) with traffic-light or numeric score
expectations.evidence: Scorecard: M=1, E=0, D=1, D=0, P=1, I=0, C=1, C=2 with RED/YELLOW status indicators

expectations.text: Applies the Champion vs Coach vs Friend distinction to the FP&A Director (likely classifies as Coach, not Champion)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly: 'FP&A Director is a Coach, not a Champion' with evidence of lacking uncomfortable actions

expectations.text: Names explicit kill criteria — the condition under which the seller will walk away
expectations.evidence: Section 3: 4 kill criteria listed with April 25 deadline (CFO 1:1, decision process, compelling event, response)

expectations.text: Provides specific actions for this week (max 3) with element, action, owner, date
expectations.evidence: Section 4: 3 actions (Tue 23 CFO email, Wed 24 FP&A coaching, Thu 25 Finance Controller discovery) with owners

expectations.text: Identifies seller-DNA derailers (e.g., Happy Ears, Alligator Arms, Single-threaded)
expectations.evidence: Seller DNA Coaching Note identifies Alligator Arms, Happy Ears, Single-threaded, Intellectual Honesty gaps

expectations.text: Interprets CFO 6-week silence through Thai cultural lens (kreng-jai = polite no, not agreement)
expectations.evidence: Section: 'Kreng-jai (เกรงใจ) — Silence means not yet...CFO may be signalling: I like the idea but I am not ready'

expectations.text: Provides overall classification: Commit / Best Case / Pipeline / Disqualify
expectations.evidence: Verdict: 'DISQUALIFY or execute emergency triage this week' with score 2/8 GREEN → recommendation

expectations.text: Includes a Win Brief artefact (why customer bought, EB, Champion, why us vs competition)
expectations.evidence: Section 1 Win Brief: Why They Bought (pain+BOI), Who Bought (CFO/EB, Controller/Champion), Why Us (speed)

expectations.text: Includes a SOW Alignment Memo mapping the 3 verbal commitments (Hardcat, data migration, training) to in-SOW / out-of-scope-promised / acknowledged status
expectations.evidence: Section 6 maps all 6 commitments: Hardcat (Conditional, SOW Line 3), data migration (YES, SOW), training (YES, SOW) with gap closure

expectations.text: Customer Success Criteria are numerical and EB-signable (e.g., '7-day close by Q4', 'BOI e-tax by Q1 2027', 'X super-users certified by Y')
expectations.evidence: Section 2: ≤7 days, live by 31 Dec 2026, ≥80% of 8 certified (≥75% exam), all with baseline/target

expectations.text: Open Commitments Log explicitly logs all 3 verbal promises with status and owner
expectations.evidence: Section 4 details all 6 promises (3 verbal + 3 outcome) with status, owner, closure plan, risk level

expectations.text: Risk Register with multiple risks (probability x impact) and named mitigations
